user.title: History Mover
user.description: Add History Mover. It adds a `history_mover.rename` action and a guided rename screen (this card's **Configure** button) that move recorder history from one entity to another.

abort.single_instance_allowed: History Mover is already set up.

single.title: Move history — single
single.description: Move the recorder history of one entity onto another.

data_description.old_entity_id: The id whose history to keep — may be an id no longer in the entity registry.
data_description.new_entity_id: The id that should own the history and keep recording into it.
data_description.on_conflict: What to do if the target already holds history of the same kind.

bulk.title: Move history — bulk
bulk.description: Move every recorder history whose id starts with the source prefix onto the same id with the target prefix. Ids no longer in the registry are included. For arbitrary mappings, use the `history_mover.rename` action with a list.

data_description.on_conflict: What to do if a target already holds history of the same kind.

confirm.title: Confirm
confirm.description:
This changes the recorder database and cannot be undone. Review, then submit to apply.

{summary}

error.same_id: Source and target must be different.
error.invalid_entity_id: Enter a valid entity ID: lower-case `domain.object_id`, for example `sensor.pv_power`.
error.empty_prefix: Enter a source prefix, for example `sensor.solax_`.
error.invalid_target: This target prefix produces invalid ids — each result must be a lower-case `domain.object_id`.
error.overlapping: The renamed ids would overlap the ids being renamed (the target prefix must not extend the source prefix). Split this into separate operations.
error.no_matches: No recorder history was found for that source prefix.
error.apply_failed: Applying the move failed or timed out. Check the Home Assistant log ('Moved history' entries show what was applied) before submitting again.

rename.name: Rename (move history)
rename.description: Moves recorder history (states and long-term statistics) from a source entity id onto a target id. If the target already has history of the same kind, it is discarded and replaced, so a replacement sensor can adopt the old history and keep recording into it.

old_entity_id.name: Source entity ID
old_entity_id.description: The id whose history you want to keep — including an id that no longer exists in the entity registry. Use with 'Target entity ID' for a single rename.

new_entity_id.name: Target entity ID
new_entity_id.description: The id that should own the history from now on. Its own existing history of the same kind is discarded (see 'On conflict').

renames.name: Renames (bulk)
renames.description: A list of pairs, each with old_entity_id and new_entity_id, to move many at once instead of a single pair.

on_conflict.name: On conflict
on_conflict.description: What to do when the target id already holds history of the same kind that the source is about to move onto it.

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: Only report what would happen (counts of moved and discarded rows). Nothing is changed. Call with 'Return response' to see the preview.

scan_references.name: Scan references
scan_references.description: After moving, report configuration files that still mention the old id (automations, scripts, scenes, dashboards, UI helpers). Nothing is edited.

options.replace: Replace — discard the target's history, then adopt the source's
options.fail: Fail — report this pair as failed
